_comment: Operational queue for the auto-writer. The scheduled function (netlify/functions/publish-blog.mjs) picks the first status:queued item in array order, writes it to Netlify Blobs, flips it to published. Titles ARE the long-tail target query (write the H1 the way people search). Strategy + keyword map: content/keyword-research.md. The first ~10 are the launch batch (highest intent across all clusters). Cadence: Mon+Thu.

topics.title: How much protein do I need a day?
topics.intent: how much protein per day / daily protein intake / grams of protein per day
topics.anchor: Sedentary adults need about 0.8 g of protein per kg of body weight; for building or keeping muscle aim higher, around 1.6 g per kg (roughly 0.7 g per pound).

topics.title: How much protein should I eat to lose weight?
topics.intent: how much protein to lose weight / protein for fat loss / protein deficit
topics.anchor: While dieting, push protein to about 1.8-2.2 g per kg of body weight to stay full and protect muscle, spread as 25-30 g per meal.

topics.title: What is leucine and why does it matter for building muscle?
topics.intent: what is leucine / leucine benefits / muscle building amino acid
topics.anchor: Leucine is the amino acid that flips on muscle protein synthesis through the mTOR pathway. Hit about 2.5-3 g of leucine in a meal and your body starts building.

topics.title: How much protein should I eat per meal to build muscle?
topics.intent: protein per meal / how much protein per sitting / 30g per meal
topics.anchor: Aim for about 20-40 g of quality protein per meal, enough to clear the ~2.5-3 g leucine threshold that maximally triggers muscle growth.

topics.title: What foods have the most protein?
topics.intent: highest protein foods / most protein / protein rich foods list
topics.anchor: Chicken breast, Greek yogurt, eggs, lean beef, fish, lentils and tofu pack the most protein per bite. Here are the real numbers.

topics.title: How do I get enough protein as a vegetarian?
topics.intent: vegetarian protein / how to get protein without meat / plant based protein
topics.anchor: Lean on soy (tofu, tempeh, edamame), beans, lentils, dairy and eggs, eat a bit more total protein, and combine sources across the day to cover all the amino acids.

topics.title: What is the leucine threshold for muscle growth?
topics.intent: leucine threshold / how much leucine per meal / leucine for muscle
topics.anchor: The leucine threshold is about 2.5 g per meal for younger adults and closer to 3 g for older adults, the dose that maximally switches on muscle protein synthesis.

topics.title: What are the best high-protein snacks for weight loss?
topics.intent: high protein snacks / protein snacks for weight loss / filling snacks
topics.anchor: Greek yogurt, cottage cheese, a tuna pouch, edamame and a scoop of whey are the most filling protein-per-calorie snacks for losing fat.

topics.title: What are the signs you're not eating enough protein?
topics.intent: not enough protein / protein deficiency signs / low protein symptoms
topics.anchor: Constant hunger, slow recovery, stalled muscle, hair thinning, weak nails and getting sick often can all point to too little protein.

topics.title: What are the cheapest high-protein foods?
topics.intent: cheap protein / budget high protein foods / protein on a budget
topics.anchor: Eggs, canned tuna, lentils, dried beans, oats, milk and peanut butter give the most protein per dollar, no supplements needed.

topics.title: How much protein is in a chicken breast?
topics.intent: protein in chicken breast / chicken protein grams / is chicken enough
topics.anchor: A medium cooked chicken breast (about 170 g) has roughly 50 g of protein, well over the leucine threshold for one meal.

topics.title: How much protein is in an egg?
topics.intent: protein in eggs / egg protein / how many eggs for protein
topics.anchor: One large egg has about 6 g of protein, so it takes roughly 3-4 eggs to reach the leucine threshold for a meal.

topics.title: How much protein is in tofu?
topics.intent: protein in tofu / tofu protein grams / is tofu high protein
topics.anchor: Firm tofu has about 9 g of protein per 100 g, so half a block (around half a cup) delivers roughly 20 g of plant protein.

topics.title: How much protein do I need to build muscle?
topics.intent: protein to build muscle / muscle gain protein / bulking protein per day
topics.anchor: About 1.6 g of protein per kg of body weight per day, split across 3-4 meals that each clear the leucine threshold, drives muscle gain.

topics.title: What foods are highest in leucine?
topics.intent: leucine rich foods / foods high in leucine / high leucine foods list
topics.anchor: Whey, eggs, chicken, beef, fish, soy and dairy are the densest leucine sources; animal proteins generally beat plants gram for gram.

topics.title: Is Greek yogurt high in protein?
topics.intent: greek yogurt protein / high protein yogurt / yogurt for muscle
topics.anchor: Plain Greek yogurt has about 10 g of protein per 100 g, so a single large 6 oz tub gives you around 18 g, most of a meal's protein.

topics.title: How can I add more protein to my meals?
topics.intent: how to add protein to meals / easy ways to eat more protein / increase protein
topics.anchor: Anchor every meal with one real protein, then stack easy add-ons: Greek yogurt, eggs, beans, a scoop of whey, or a handful of nuts.

topics.title: Can you eat too much protein?
topics.intent: too much protein / protein side effects / is high protein bad for kidneys
topics.anchor: For healthy people, high protein is safe; beyond about 2.2 g per kg there's little extra muscle benefit, so the rest is just calories.

topics.title: Should I drink a protein shake before or after a workout?
topics.intent: protein shake before or after workout / when to drink protein / timing
topics.anchor: It barely matters. Total daily protein drives results, so have your shake whenever fits, ideally within a couple of hours of training.

topics.title: What's a high-protein breakfast?
topics.intent: high protein breakfast / protein in the morning / breakfast for muscle
topics.anchor: Aim for 25-40 g at breakfast, eggs, Greek yogurt, cottage cheese or a shake, to start the day above the leucine threshold.

topics.title: How much protein is in peanut butter?
topics.intent: protein in peanut butter / peanut butter protein grams / is peanut butter high protein
topics.anchor: Peanut butter has about 4 g of protein per tablespoon (around 24 g per 100 g), good as an add-on but not a complete protein on its own.

topics.title: How much protein is in lentils and beans?
topics.intent: protein in lentils / beans protein / legume protein grams
topics.anchor: Half a cup of cooked lentils has about 9 g of protein (17 g per cup); most cooked beans land around 7-8 g per half cup.

topics.title: What are the best vegan protein sources?
topics.intent: best vegan protein / highest protein vegan foods / plant protein ranked
topics.anchor: Soy (tofu, tempeh, edamame), seitan, lentils, beans and pea protein lead the pack for plant-based protein density.

topics.title: How much protein do women need?
topics.intent: protein for women / how much protein female / women weight loss protein
topics.anchor: The same ratios apply: about 1.6 g per kg to build muscle and 1.8-2.2 g per kg while dieting to stay full and hold muscle.

topics.title: How much protein do older adults need?
topics.intent: protein for seniors / protein for elderly / protein over 50
topics.anchor: Older adults need more, not less, about 1.2-1.6 g per kg, with closer to 3 g of leucine per meal to fight age-related muscle loss.

topics.title: Leucine vs BCAAs: what's the difference?
topics.intent: leucine vs bcaa / are bcaas worth it / bcaa for muscle
topics.anchor: Leucine is the one BCAA that actually triggers muscle growth; whole protein or a leucine-rich meal beats a BCAA supplement for most people.

topics.title: Is a protein shake as good as real food?
topics.intent: protein shake vs food / is whey worth it / shake or meal
topics.anchor: Whole food wins for fullness and nutrients; shakes win for speed. Both build muscle if they hit the leucine threshold.

topics.title: What's the difference between complete and incomplete protein?
topics.intent: complete vs incomplete protein / amino acid profile / do I need complete proteins
topics.anchor: A complete protein has all nine essential amino acids in good amounts. Eat varied plants across the day and you cover the gaps.

topics.title: Is cottage cheese high in protein?
topics.intent: cottage cheese protein / protein in cottage cheese / cottage cheese for muscle
topics.anchor: Cottage cheese has about 11 g of protein per 100 g and is rich in slow-digesting casein, making it a strong protein choice.

topics.title: Should I eat protein before bed?
topics.intent: protein before bed / casein at night / nighttime protein for muscle
topics.anchor: A slow protein like casein or cottage cheese before sleep can feed overnight muscle repair, useful if you train hard.

topics.title: Whey vs plant protein powder: which is better?
topics.intent: whey vs plant protein / best protein powder / vegan protein powder
topics.anchor: Whey is the leucine king, but a soy or blended pea-rice powder gets you there too. Pick what fits your diet and your gut.

topics.title: How do I know how much protein is in my meal?
topics.intent: how much protein in a meal / estimate protein / count protein in food
topics.anchor: Eyeball the protein source: a palm-sized portion of meat or fish is roughly 25-30 g. Or snap a photo and let a tool estimate it.

topics.title: What are the most common protein myths?
topics.intent: protein myths / protein misconceptions / debunked protein facts
topics.anchor: You can use more than 30 g in a meal, protein won't wreck healthy kidneys, and plant eaters can build plenty of muscle.

topics.title: Do runners and endurance athletes need protein?
topics.intent: protein for runners / endurance protein / cardio protein needs
topics.anchor: Yes. Endurance athletes need about 1.2-1.6 g per kg to repair muscle and adapt, not just carbs.

topics.title: Is it better to spread protein across the day?
topics.intent: protein timing / spread protein evenly / protein per meal vs total
topics.anchor: Spreading protein into 3-4 meals that each hit the leucine threshold beats loading it all into one big dinner.

topics.title: What are the best lean protein foods for cutting calories?
topics.intent: lean protein / low calorie high protein foods / cutting foods
topics.anchor: Chicken breast, white fish, egg whites, nonfat Greek yogurt and tofu give the most protein for the fewest calories.

topics.title: Does eating more protein help you lose belly fat?
topics.intent: protein for belly fat / does protein burn fat / protein and fat loss
topics.anchor: Protein doesn't target belly fat directly, but it keeps you full, protects muscle, and burns more calories to digest, helping overall fat loss in a deficit.
